title: Ratchet test coverage thresholds as tests are added
description:
Current coverage thresholds are floors, not targets:
- iOS: 30% (low - should increase as we fix bugs and add UI tests)
- macOS: 20% (low - 295 tests passing but coverage spread thin)
- NetMonitorCore: 40%
- NetworkScanKit: 38%
Plan: after each test-writing session, bump the gate by 5% to prevent regression.
Coverage script: scripts/coverage/collect-and-gate.sh
Config: docs/testing/coverage-gates.md

macOS target is missing NSLocalNetworkUsageDescription (required for NWListener/Bonjour companion service on macOS 15+) and NSBonjourServices (should declare _netmon._tcp). Without these, companion service may silently fail or app may be rejected.

description:
CRASH on startup: SwiftData fatal error decoding MonitoringTarget.targetProtocol.
Stored value: 'HTTPS' (uppercase), enum expects: 'https' (lowercase).

Error: Cannot initialize TargetProtocol from invalid String value HTTPS

Root cause: Persistent container (or test data) has uppercase 'HTTPS' stored from a previous data format. TargetProtocol enum uses lowercase raw values (icmp, tcp, http, https).

Impact: ALL unit tests fail — the host app crashes before tests can run.

Fix options:
1. Add case-insensitive init to TargetProtocol.init(rawValue:) that maps HTTPS → https
2. Add SwiftData VersionedSchema migration  
3. Use in-memory SwiftData store for tests (launch arg --uitesting already clears some things)

Short-term workaround: Delete corrupt SwiftData store in test container.
close_reason: Fixed by adding case-insensitive Codable init to TargetProtocol. Handles legacy uppercase HTTPS values from persistent store. 289 unit tests passing.
title: P0: SwiftData crash decoding TargetProtocol uppercase 'HTTPS' from legacy container

ShellPingParser: statsLineWithRttPrefixInsteadOfRoundTrip fails — mdev vs stddev mismatch
close_reason: Fixed statsPattern regex to accept both stddev (macOS) and mdev (Linux) in ping stats output
description: Pre-existing failure in ShellPingParserExtendedTests.swift:149-150. Test uses 'rtt min/avg/max/mdev = 5.0/5.0/5.0/0.0 ms' but ShellPingService.statsPattern only matches 'min/avg/max/stddev = ...'. macOS ping uses 'round-trip ... stddev'; Linux ping uses 'rtt ... mdev'. Fix options: [x] Option A: change test data from mdev→stddev (if parser is macOS-only) [x] Option B: update parser to accept both stddev and mdev (better cross-platform support) Failing: result.minLatency→0.0 expected 5.0, result.avgLatency→0.0 expected 5.0. Pre-existing since d365c86.

description: SwiftData migration fails on launch when upgrading from a store without LocalDevice.isGateway. Error: 'Validation error missing attribute values on mandatory destination attribute (entity=LocalDevice, attribute=isGateway)'. Store: ~/Library/Containers/com.netmonitor.NetMonitor/Data/Library/Application Support/default.store. Reproduces in test host and likely in production upgrades. Fix: add @Attribute(defaultValue: false) to isGateway, or define a VersionedSchema migration plan with a mapping policy that sets isGateway=false for existing rows. isGateway added in fe95dc5.
close_reason: Added default values (false) to isGateway and supportsWakeOnLan stored properties for SwiftData migration compatibility
title: CoreData migration failure: LocalDevice.isGateway requires migration policy or defaultValue

title: Triage Periphery dead code warnings (117 baseline)
description:
Periphery scan found 117 unused declaration warnings at baseline. Triage needed:
- Identify real dead code from 1.0→2.0 migration (remove it)
- Mark SwiftData/Codable false positives with // periphery:ignore
- Mark protocol conformance false positives
- Goal: reduce to <20 actionable warnings
See .periphery.yml for config and docs/agent-readiness/03-session-code-quality-analysis.md for context.
